This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Adil Jussawalla papers
Jussawalla, Adil, 1940-
Clearing House
Bombay Poets Archive
8519
13.5 cubic feet.
1944-2019 Collection material in English Includes correspondence, published material, and several manuscripts. Correspondence, diaries, manuscripts, newspapers, scrapbooks. <<<<<<< HEAD 11 12 13 ======= 11 12 13 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 9150046
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Jussawalla is an Indian poet, critic, and a man of letters. Together with Arvind Krishna Mehrotra he founded the Clearing House press that brought literary modernism to India.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence, published material, and several manuscripts.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
November 2017
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, November 2017
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, November 2017
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use
Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-10929, TR-10930.
Cite As:
Adil Jussawalla Papers, #8519. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
RELATED MATERIALS
Related material located in collection #8511, Arvind Krishna Mehrotra papers.
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Sarang, Vilas. Seven Critical Essays , Undated
Mahapatra, Jayanta. The False Start , 1980
Nazareth, H.O. Lobo , 1984
Menen, Rajendar. Nature Cure: Dr. J.M. Jussawalla's Drugless Healing , 2013

Ed. Bhalla, Alok; Verma, Nirmal; Ananthamurthy, U. R. Yatra 2 , 1994
Pritish Nandy, TR. The Poetry of Kaifi Azmi , 1975
Bandekar, Prakash. I am India' and a Few Other Poems , Undated
Chitre, Dilip; Jussawalla, Adil. The Reasoning Vision: Jehangir Sabavala's Painterly Universe , 1980
Nazareth, H.O. Lobo
Mehrotra, Arvind Krishna. Distance in Statute Miles
Lal, P. The Parrot's Death and Other Poems
de Souza, Eunice . Women in Dutch Painting
Patel, Gieve. How Do You Withstand, Body
Jussawalla, Adil. Missing Person
Patel, Gieve. Mister Behram
Mistry, Cyrus. Doongaji House
Shetty, Manohar. Borrowed Time
Shivdasani, Menka. Nirvana at Ten Rupees
Lal, P. Love's the First
Rehman, Tariq. Work and other Short Stories
Vatsyayana, S. H. Prison Days and Other Poems
Khabardar, Ardeshir F. The Silken Tassel
Jhabvala, S. H. Poems: Written in Prison
Sett, Adi K. Rain in My Heart
Chander, Krishan. Seven Faces of London
Malgonkar, Manohar. Bombay Beware
Rahman, Tariq. A History of Pakistani Literature in English
Fernandes, Keith and Eunice de Souza (Editors) . Bequest
Opinion Literary Quarterly Winter Issue
Opinion Literary Quarterly Autumn Issue
Opinion Literary Quarterly Spring Issue
Dhondy, Farrukh and Diane Troy. Carcanet
Measure 3: Young Poets of India ,
Nandy, Pritish (Translator). Poems from Bangladesh
Anstey, F. Baboo Jabberjee
Katrak, K. D. Purgatory: Songs from The Holy Planet
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. The Map of the World
Banerjea, Pradeep (Translator). Some Post-Independence Bengali Poems
Shungloo, Krishan. The Night is Heavy
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. The Sleeper Awakened
Chattopadhyaya, Harindranath. Edgeways and the Saint

Acharya, Sri Ananda. Snow-Birds
Malgonkar, Manohar. Rumble-Tumble

Katrak, K. D. Underworld
Tripathi, Govardhanram Madhavram. Scrap Book

Hutheesing, Krishna. With No Regrets
Pillai, G. Parameswaran. London and Paris through Indian Spectacles
Kohli, Devindra (Editor). Indian Writers at Work
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence
Series II. Writer Files
Series III. Literary Work by Adil Jussawalla
Series IV. Writing by Others
Series V. Personal Writing and Documents
Series VI. Tape Recordings
Series VII. Assorted Files
Criticism, interpretation, etc. Poetry. Indic literature (English) -- History and criticism. India -- In literature.
